records.text:
Expected outcome
Projects are expected to contribute to ALL of the following outcomes:
- Demonstrated cost reduction of green hydrogen production at industrial scale,
  with a credible pathway to below EUR 3/kg by 2030.
- Increased European supply-chain resilience for electrolyser components.
- Validated integration of electrolysis with variable renewable electricity,
  reducing curtailment.

metadata.title: SYNTHETIC — Scalable green hydrogen production for hard-to-abate industry
metadata.heading: Expected outcome

records.text:
Scope
Proposals should demonstrate, in a relevant industrial environment, an integrated
green-hydrogen production system coupling renewable electricity with advanced
electrolysis. Activities should address durability, dynamic operation under
variable load, and lifecycle sustainability. Consortia should include at least one
industrial off-taker from a hard-to-abate sector (steel, cement, chemicals).

metadata.title: SYNTHETIC — Scalable green hydrogen production for hard-to-abate industry
metadata.heading: Scope

records.text:
Strategic framing (why the funder cares)
This topic operationalises the REPowerEU and EU Hydrogen Strategy goals. Evaluators
will reward proposals that explicitly connect technical results to EU-level energy
sovereignty, decarbonisation of hard-to-abate sectors, and industrial competitiveness
against non-EU electrolyser manufacturers. Strong proposals quantify the contribution
to the 2030 renewable-hydrogen targets and articulate the policy relevance, not just
the technology.

metadata.title: SYNTHETIC — Scalable green hydrogen production for hard-to-abate industry
metadata.heading: Strategic framing (why the funder cares)

records.text:
Cross-cutting priorities to address
- Open science and FAIR data management.
- Gender dimension in the research/innovation content.
- "Do No Significant Harm" and lifecycle/environmental sustainability.
- A credible exploitation and standardisation strategy.

metadata.title: SYNTHETIC — Scalable green hydrogen production for hard-to-abate industry
metadata.heading: Cross-cutting priorities to address

records.text:
Horizon Europe — Standard Application Form (Part B) structure
Horizon Europe Research and Innovation Actions (RIA) and Innovation Actions (IA)
are evaluated against three award criteria, each scored 0–5 (threshold 3, overall
threshold 10). Part B has a 45-page limit for sections 1–3.

metadata.title: Horizon Europe RIA/IA Proposal Template (Part B)
metadata.heading: Horizon Europe — Standard Application Form (Part B) structure

records.text:
Section 1 — Excellence
[section: excellence]
Covers: objectives and ambition; relation to the work programme; concept and
methodology; how the project goes beyond the state of the art.

Evaluation focus:
- Clarity and pertinence of the project's objectives, and the extent to which they
  are ambitious and go beyond the state of the art.
- Soundness of the methodology, including consideration of inter/multidisciplinary
  approaches, gender dimension in research content, and open science practices.

Required elements:
1.1 Objectives and ambition (measurable, verifiable, linked to call scope)
1.2 Methodology (research approach, work concept, risk-aware design)
1.3 Beyond state of the art (clear baseline + quantified advance)

metadata.title: Horizon Europe RIA/IA Proposal Template (Part B)
metadata.heading: Section 1 — Excellence

records.text:
Section 2 — Impact
[section: impact]
Covers: the project's pathways towards impact; the scale and significance of
expected outcomes; measures to maximise impact (dissemination, exploitation,
communication).

Evaluation focus:
- Credibility of the pathways to achieve the expected outcomes and impacts in the
  work programme, and the likely scale and significance of the contributions.
- Suitability and quality of the measures to maximise expected outcomes and impacts.

Required elements:
2.1 Project's pathways towards impact (outcomes -> impacts, with assumptions)
2.2 Measures to maximise impact — dissemination, exploitation, IPR, communication
2.3 Summary canvas (specific needs, expected results, target groups, outcomes, impacts)

metadata.title: Horizon Europe RIA/IA Proposal Template (Part B)
metadata.heading: Section 2 — Impact

records.text:
Section 3 — Quality and efficiency of the implementation
[section: implementation]
Covers: work plan and work packages; allocation of resources; consortium capacity.

Evaluation focus:
- Quality and effectiveness of the work plan, assessment of risks, and
  appropriateness of the effort assigned to work packages.
- Capacity and role of each participant, and the extent the consortium as a whole
  brings together the necessary expertise.

Required elements:
3.1 Work plan and resources (WP list, deliverables, milestones, Gantt, PM table)
3.2 Capacity of participants and consortium as a whole

metadata.title: Horizon Europe RIA/IA Proposal Template (Part B)
metadata.heading: Section 3 — Quality and efficiency of the implementation

records.text:
Mandatory annexes / checks
- Ethics self-assessment, security, gender equality plan (eligibility for many
  beneficiaries), open science, data management (DMP if data generated).

metadata.title: Horizon Europe RIA/IA Proposal Template (Part B)
metadata.heading: Mandatory annexes / checks

records.text:
Exemplar 1 — Objectives and ambition (scored 5/5)
The overall objective of HYDRA-FLEX is to demonstrate a dynamically operated 50 MW
PEM electrolysis system that produces renewable hydrogen at EUR 2.8/kg by project
end, validated in a steel-plant environment. This objective is decomposed into four
SMART objectives, each with a quantified target, a verification method, and a
responsible work package:

- O1: Raise stack durability from 40,000 h to 60,000 h under dynamic load (verified
  by accelerated stress testing, WP2).
- O2: Cut iridium loading by 70% versus the 2024 baseline of 1.5 mg/cm² (verified by
  XRF and performance retention, WP3).
- O3: Demonstrate <2 s ramp response to a 0–100% load step (verified on the pilot,
  WP4).
- O4: Reduce levelised cost of hydrogen to EUR 2.8/kg (verified by an audited
  techno-economic model, WP5).

Ambition is expressed against a clearly quantified state of the art: today's best
demonstrated systems achieve EUR 4.5/kg at 40,000 h durability with 1.5 mg/cm²
iridium loading. HYDRA-FLEX advances all three dimensions simultaneously, which no
prior project has achieved.

metadata.title: SYNTHETIC — Exemplar Excellence section passages (funded projects)
metadata.heading: Exemplar 1 — Objectives and ambition (scored 5/5)

records.text:
Exemplar 2 — Beyond the state of the art (scored 5/5)
We summarise the advance in a single table mapping each innovation to the current
baseline, the project target, and the quantified delta. Rather than claiming general
"novelty", we tie each claim to a measurable KPI and cite the specific limitation in
the current literature it overcomes. The methodology explicitly integrates the gender
dimension by analysing how operator interface design affects safety outcomes across
a gender-balanced operator cohort, and adopts open-science practices by committing to
publish all non-confidential datasets under FAIR principles.

metadata.title: SYNTHETIC — Exemplar Excellence section passages (funded projects)
metadata.heading: Exemplar 2 — Beyond the state of the art (scored 5/5)

records.text:
Exemplar 1 — Pathways towards impact (scored 5/5)
We articulate impact as an explicit chain: project results -> outcomes -> impacts,
stating the assumptions at each step. For example: the validated low-iridium stack
(result) enables European manufacturers to produce electrolysers with 70% less
critical raw material (outcome), which contributes to EU supply-chain resilience and
the 2030 renewable-hydrogen target (impact). Each link names the target group (steel
off-takers, electrolyser OEMs, EU policymakers) and the barrier the project removes.

Crucially, we connect technical KPIs to the funder's strategic intent: by quantifying
the contribution to the REPowerEU hydrogen target (0.4 Mt/year potential at scale) we
demonstrate policy relevance, not just technical merit. Reviewers of this topic
reward proposals that make the EU-level significance explicit.

metadata.title: SYNTHETIC — Exemplar Impact section passages (funded projects)
metadata.heading: Exemplar 1 — Pathways towards impact (scored 5/5)

records.text:
Exemplar 2 — Measures to maximise impact (scored 5/5)
The dissemination, exploitation and communication (DEC) plan is presented as a table
with concrete channels, audiences, KPIs, timing and responsible partners. Exploitation
includes a freedom-to-operate analysis, a joint IPR agreement summarised in the
consortium agreement, and three named key exploitable results each with an owner and
a route to market. A standardisation strategy commits to contributing to two ISO/IEC
working groups. Communication targets quantified reach with measurable indicators
rather than vague "we will raise awareness" statements.

metadata.title: SYNTHETIC — Exemplar Impact section passages (funded projects)
metadata.heading: Exemplar 2 — Measures to maximise impact (scored 5/5)

records.text:
Exemplar 1 — Work plan and resources (scored 5/5)
The work plan comprises six work packages with clear interdependencies shown in a
PERT chart. Each WP has SMART objectives, a lead beneficiary, listed deliverables and
milestones with verifiable acceptance criteria, and effort justified in person-months.
A dedicated risk table lists each critical risk, its likelihood/severity, the WP that
owns it, and a concrete mitigation with a fallback. Effort allocation is justified
against the ambition: 40% to the pilot demonstration WP, reflecting that the IA's
value lies in validation in a relevant environment.

metadata.title: SYNTHETIC — Exemplar Implementation section passages (funded projects)
metadata.heading: Exemplar 1 — Work plan and resources (scored 5/5)

records.text:
Exemplar 2 — Consortium capacity (scored 5/5)
The consortium is presented as complementary rather than a list of CVs. A capability
matrix maps each required competence (electrolyser stacks, power electronics, steel
process integration, techno-economic modelling, exploitation) to the partner that
provides it, demonstrating no gaps and no redundancy. The inclusion of an industrial
off-taker from a hard-to-abate sector is highlighted as directly responding to the
call's eligibility expectation. Roles are non-overlapping and each partner's
contribution is essential to the project objectives.

metadata.title: SYNTHETIC — Exemplar Implementation section passages (funded projects)
metadata.heading: Exemplar 2 — Consortium capacity (scored 5/5)

records.text:
Common reviewer weaknesses this section avoids
- Effort not justified against WP ambition (reviewers flag generic PM distribution).
- Risk tables that list risks without owners or concrete mitigations.
- Consortium described as CVs rather than complementary capability coverage.
- Deliverables without verifiable acceptance criteria.

metadata.title: SYNTHETIC — Exemplar Implementation section passages (funded projects)
metadata.heading: Common reviewer weaknesses this section avoids
